research_series.series_name: Hydration, Electrolytes, Blood Pressure, Orthostatic Intolerance, Dizziness, Fatigue, and Cognition

patterns.pattern_name: Dehydration/hypohydration impairs sustained attention and increases fatigue/mood disturbance

mediator.name: hydration_status
mediator.pathway: fluid deficit and increased osmolality reduce physiological reserve and may affect cerebral perfusion/attention systems
mediator.mechanism: hypohydration increases subjective fatigue and impairs vigilance/sustained attention, especially above ~2% body mass loss or under heat/exercise stress

inference_rule.plain_language_rule: Objective dehydration or hypohydration increases likelihood of subjective fatigue/mood disturbance and impaired sustained attention, especially with >2% body mass loss or physiological stress.

hypotheses.reason: Meta-analyses and RCTs show moderate-to-large impairments in attention/vigilance under objective dehydration, with stronger effects at greater fluid deficits.

evidence.summary: Dehydration impairs sustained attention/vigilance and increases subjective fatigue/mood disturbance, especially when measured objectively and under heat/exercise stress.

limitations: Some cognitive domains show null effects at mild dehydration in resting healthy adults.
Single-point hydration markers may miss dynamic compensation.

patterns.pattern_name: Orthostatic intolerance/POTS links volume/BP dysregulation to dizziness, brain fog, fatigue, and cognitive impairment

mediator.name: cerebral_hypoperfusion
mediator.pathway: volume depletion or autonomic dysregulation causes impaired orthostatic compensation and reduced cerebral perfusion
mediator.mechanism: standing challenge or tilt produces excessive HR/BP changes and cerebral perfusion reduction, driving dizziness, fatigue, brain fog and working memory/executive dysfunction

inference_rule.plain_language_rule: Orthostatic intolerance or POTS-pattern physiology increases likelihood of dizziness, brain fog, fatigue, and cognitive impairment through cerebral hypoperfusion/autonomic compensation failure.

hypotheses.reason: Orthostatic/POTS studies support a mechanistic volume/BP/cerebral perfusion pathway; symptom relief with volume expansion/rehydration supports reversibility.

evidence.summary: Orthostatic intolerance/POTS provides mechanistic support for volume/BP/cerebral perfusion links to dizziness, fatigue, brain fog, and cognitive dysfunction.

limitations: Cerebral perfusion is not always measured in routine studies.

negative_or_uncertain_findings.claim: Mild dehydration has little/no effect on some cognitive domains in healthy adults at rest.
negative_or_uncertain_findings.reason: Some large cohort/meta-analysis/null findings suggest limited impact outside stress or extreme deficit contexts.

negative_or_uncertain_findings.claim: Electrolyte imbalance directly explains everyday fatigue/cognition outside clinical extremes.
negative_or_uncertain_findings.reason: Associations are stronger in acute illness/TBI/stroke, but causality is confounded by disease severity.

high_value_candidate_nodes.definition: Reduced body water status measured by objective hydration markers or body mass loss.
Symptoms and abnormal cardiovascular response provoked by upright posture.
Excessive orthostatic heart rate increase with orthostatic symptoms, not defined by low BP alone.
Reduced cerebral blood flow/perfusion, especially during orthostatic or volume stress.

research_gaps: Mediation analyses linking hydration/electrolyte/orthostatic physiology to cerebral perfusion and neurobehavioral outcomes are limited.
Longitudinal intervention data on hydration and cognitive decline are limited.
Sex and age moderators are under-characterized.

most_supported_patterns: Dehydration/hypohydration impairs sustained attention/vigilance and increases fatigue/mood disturbance.
Orthostatic intolerance/POTS can produce dizziness, brain fog, fatigue, and cognitive impairment via cerebral hypoperfusion.
highest_value_patterns_for_inference: Normal seated BP does not exclude orthostatic intolerance.
Hydration effects are strongest under stress/extreme deficit contexts and for sustained attention.
patterns_requiring_caution: Mild dehydration at rest may have limited effects on some cognitive domains.
Electrolyte imbalance evidence outside clinical extremes is confounded.
recommended_next_queries: How do physical activity, exercise load, lactate, overtraining, and recovery markers affect fatigue and cognition?
How do thyroid markers and basal metabolism affect fatigue, mood, and cognition?
summary.overall_assessment: Hydration/orthostatic physiology is a strong practical inference layer for fatigue, dizziness, brain fog, and sustained attention, especially when combined with heat/exercise stress or orthostatic challenge data.
